items.body: Routing to the team for assistance: `@ArthurMa1978`

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** This is a bug report for `Azure.ResourceManager.Network 1.14.0` where `ApplicationGatewayData.EnableFips` always returns `null` after enabling FIPS mode on an Application Gateway. The root cause appears to be a case sensitivity mismatch: the API response uses the key `enableFIPS` (uppercase "FIPS"), but the SDK property deserializes using `enableFips` (mixed case), causing the value to never be populated.

<details>
<summary>🔍 Issue Analysis</summary>

**Root Cause Hypothesis:**
The JSON deserialization logic in the generated `ApplicationGatewayData` class likely uses a case-sensitive key match. The Azure REST API returns `enableFIPS`, but the SDK may be looking for `enableFips`, resulting in the value being silently dropped during deserialization.

**Reproduction Path:**
1. Enable FIPS mode on an Azure Application Gateway
2. Call `GetAsync()` on the `ApplicationGatewayResource`
3. Check `.Data.EnableFips` — it will be `null` despite FIPS being enabled

**Suggested Debugging Steps:**
- Capture the raw HTTP response (e.g., via Fiddler or Azure SDK logging) and confirm the exact JSON key casing returned by the API
- Compare with the deserialization code in the generated file (likely in `sdk/network/Azure.ResourceManager.Network/src/Generated/Models/ApplicationGatewayData.Serialization.cs`)
- Check for `enableFIPS` vs `enableFips` in the serialization switch/if statements

</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Prediction Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — confirmed; the package `Azure.ResourceManager.Network` is a management-plane SDK
- **Service:** `Network - Application Gateway` — confirmed; the bug is specifically in `ApplicationGatewayData`, part of the Application Gateway resource within the Network SDK
- **Confidence:** High — validated against a quality reference issue [#38402](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/38402) which uses the same `Mgmt` + `Network - Application Gateway` label combination for a similar Azure.ResourceManager.Network / ApplicationGateway issue

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [Azure.ResourceManager.Network package source](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/network/Azure.ResourceManager.Network)
- [ApplicationGateway REST API reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)
- [Azure SDK for .NET contributing guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

</details>
